texts.NKJV: traitors, headstrong, haughty, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God,
texts.DRB: Traitors, stubborn, puffed up, and lovers of pleasures more than of God:
texts.ESV: treacherous, reckless, swollen with conceit, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God,
texts.LEB: traitors, reckless, conceited, loving pleasure rather than loving God,
texts.AMP: traitors, reckless, conceited, lovers of [sensual] pleasure rather than lovers of God,
texts.NLT: They will betray their friends, be reckless, be puffed up with pride, and love pleasure rather than God.
texts.AKJV: traitors, heady, highminded, lovers of pleasures more than lovers of God;
texts.NRSV: treacherous, reckless, swollen with conceit, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God,

texts.NIV84: having a form of godliness but denying its power. Have nothing to do with them.
texts.KJV: Having a form of godliness, but denying the power thereof: from such turn away.
texts.NASB95: holding to a form of godliness, although they have denied its power; Avoid such men as these.
texts.NKJV: having a form of godliness but denying its power. And from such people turn away!
texts.DRB: Having an appearance indeed of godliness, but denying the power thereof. Now these avoid.
texts.ESV: having the appearance of godliness, but denying its power. Avoid such people.
texts.LEB: maintaining a form of godliness, but denying its power. Avoid these people.
texts.AMP: holding to a form of [outward] godliness (religion), although they have denied its power [for their conduct nullifies their claim of faith]. Avoid such people and keep far away from them.
texts.NLT: They will act religious, but they will reject the power that could make them godly. Stay away from people like that!
texts.AKJV: having a form of godliness, but denying the power thereof: from such turn away.
texts.NRSV: holding to the outward form of godliness but denying its power. Avoid them!

texts.NIV84: They are the kind who worm their way into homes and gain control over weak-willed women, who are loaded down with sins and are swayed by all kinds of evil desires,
texts.KJV: For of this sort are they which creep into houses, and lead captive silly women laden with sins, led away with divers lusts,
texts.NASB95: For among them are those who enter into households and captivate weak women weighed down with sins, led on by various impulses,
texts.NKJV: For of this sort are those who creep into households and make captives of gullible women loaded down with sins, led away by various lusts,
texts.DRB: For of these sort are they who creep into houses, and lead captive silly women laden with sins, who are led away with divers desires:
texts.ESV: For among them are those who creep into households and capture weak women, burdened with sins and led astray by various passions,
texts.LEB: For from these are those who slip into houses and captivate foolish women loaded down with sins, led by various kinds of desires,
texts.AMP: For among them are those who worm their way into homes and captivate morally weak and spiritually-dwarfed women weighed down by [the burden of their] sins, easily swayed by various impulses,
texts.NLT: They are the kind who work their way into people’s homes and win the confidence of vulnerable women who are burdened with the guilt of sin and controlled by various desires.
texts.AKJV: For of this sort are they which creep into houses, and lead captive silly women laden with sins, led away with divers lusts,
texts.NRSV: For among them are those who make their way into households and captivate immature women, overwhelmed by their sins and swayed by all kinds of desires,

texts.NIV84: always learning but never able to acknowledge the truth.
texts.KJV: Ever learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
texts.NASB95: always learning and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
texts.NKJV: always learning and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
texts.DRB: Ever learning, and never attaining to the knowledge of the truth.
texts.ESV: always learning and never able to arrive at a knowledge of the truth.
texts.LEB: always learning and never able to come to a knowledge of the truth.
texts.AMP: always learning and listening to anybody who will teach them, but never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
texts.NLT: (Such women are forever following new teachings, but they are never able to understand the truth.)
texts.AKJV: ever learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
texts.NRSV: who are always studying yet never able to recognize truth.

texts.NIV84: Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so also these men oppose the truth--men of depraved minds, who, as far as the faith is concerned, are rejected.
texts.KJV: Now as Jannes and Jambres withstood Moses, so do these also resist the truth: men of corrupt minds, reprobate concerning the faith.
texts.NASB95: Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so these men also oppose the truth, men of depraved mind, rejected in regard to the faith.
texts.NKJV: Now as Jannes and Jambres resisted Moses, so do these also resist the truth: men of corrupt minds, disapproved concerning the faith;
texts.DRB: Now as Jannes and Mambres resisted Moses, so these also resist the truth, men corrupted in mind, reprobate concerning the faith.
texts.ESV: Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so these men also oppose the truth, men corrupted in mind and disqualified regarding the faith.
texts.LEB: And just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so also these oppose the truth, people corrupted in mind, disqualified concerning the faith.
texts.AMP: Just as Jannes and Jambres [the court magicians of Egypt] opposed Moses, so these men also oppose the truth, men of depraved mind, unqualified and worthless [as teachers] in regard to the faith.
texts.NLT: These teachers oppose the truth just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses. They have depraved minds and a counterfeit faith.
texts.AKJV: Now as Jannes and Jambres withstood Moses, so do these also resist the truth: men of corrupt minds, reprobate concerning the faith.
texts.NRSV: As Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so these people, of corrupt mind and counterfeit faith, also oppose the truth.

texts.NIV84: But they will not get very far because, as in the case of those men, their folly will be clear to everyone.
texts.KJV: But they shall proceed no further: for their folly shall be manifest unto all men, as their's also was.
texts.NASB95: But they will not make further progress; for their folly will be obvious to all, just as Jannes’s and Jambres’s folly was also.
texts.NKJV: but they will progress no further, for their folly will be manifest to all, as theirs also was.
texts.DRB: But they shall proceed no farther; for their folly shall be manifest to all men, as theirs also was.
texts.ESV: But they will not get very far, for their folly will be plain to all, as was that of those two men.
texts.LEB: But they will not progress to a greater extent, for their folly will be quite evident to everyone, as also the folly of those two was.
texts.AMP: But they will not get very far, for their meaningless nonsense and ignorance will become obvious to everyone, as was that of Jannes and Jambres.
texts.NLT: But they won’t get away with this for long. Someday everyone will recognize what fools they are, just as with Jannes and Jambres.
texts.AKJV: But they shall proceed no further: for their folly shall be manifest unto all men, as theirs also was.
texts.NRSV: But they will not make much progress because, as in the case of those two men, their folly will become plain to everyone.

texts.NIV84: And do this, understanding the present time. The hour has come for you to wake up from your slumber, because our salvation is nearer now than when we first believed.
texts.KJV: And that, knowing the time, that now it is high time to awake out of sleep: for now is our salvation nearer than when we believed.
texts.NASB95: Do this, knowing the time, that it is already the hour for you to awaken from sleep; for now salvation is nearer to us than when we believed.
texts.NKJV: And do this, knowing the time, that now it is high time to awake out of sleep; for now our salvation is nearer than when we first believed.
texts.DRB: And that knowing the season; that it is now the hour for us to rise from sleep. For now our salvation is nearer than when we believed.
texts.ESV: Besides this you know the time, that the hour has come for you to wake from sleep. For salvation is nearer to us now than when we first believed.
texts.LEB: And do this because you know the time, that it is already the hour for you to wake up from sleep. For our salvation is nearer now than when we believed.
texts.AMP: Do this, knowing that this is a critical time. It is already the hour for you to awaken from your sleep [of spiritual complacency]; for our salvation is nearer to us now than when we first believed [in Christ].
texts.NLT: This is all the more urgent, for you know how late it is; time is running out. Wake up, for our salvation is nearer now than when we first believed.
texts.AKJV: And that, knowing the time, that now it is high time to awake out of sleep: for now is our salvation nearer than when we believed.
texts.NRSV: Besides this, you know what time it is, how it is already the moment for you to wake from sleep. For salvation is nearer to us now than when we became believers;

texts.NIV84: The night is nearly over; the day is almost here. So let us put aside the deeds of darkness and put on the armor of light.
texts.KJV: The night is far spent, the day is at hand: let us therefore cast off the works of darkness, and let us put on the armour of light.
texts.NASB95: The night is almost gone, and the day is near. Therefore let us lay aside the deeds of darkness and put on the armor of light.
texts.NKJV: The night is far spent, the day is at hand. Therefore let us cast off the works of darkness, and let us put on the armor of light.
texts.DRB: The night is passed, and the day is at hand. Let us therefore cast off the works of darkness, and put on the armour of light.
texts.ESV: The night is far gone; the day is at hand. So then let us cast off the works of darkness and put on the armor of light.
texts.LEB: The night is far gone, and the day has drawn near. Therefore let us throw off the deeds of darkness and put on the weapons of light.
texts.AMP: The night [this present evil age] is almost gone and the day [of Christ’s return] is almost here. So let us fling away the works of darkness and put on the [full] armor of light.
texts.NLT: The night is almost gone; the day of salvation will soon be here. So remove your dark deeds like dirty clothes, and put on the shining armor of right living.
texts.AKJV: The night is far spent, the day is at hand: let us therefore cast off the works of darkness, and let us put on the armour of light.
texts.NRSV: the night is far gone; the day is near. Let us then throw off the works of darkness and put on the armor of light;

texts.NIV84: Let us behave decently, as in the daytime, not in orgies and drunkenness, not in sexual immorality and debauchery, not in dissension and jealousy.
texts.KJV: Let us walk honestly, as in the day; not in rioting and drunkenness, not in chambering and wantonness, not in strife and envying.
texts.NASB95: Let us behave properly as in the day, not in carousing and drunkenness, not in sexual promiscuity and sensuality, not in strife and jealousy.
texts.NKJV: Let us walk properly, as in the day, not in revelry and drunkenness, not in lewdness and lust, not in strife and envy.
texts.DRB: Let us walk honestly, as in the day: not in rioting and drunkenness, not in chambering and impurities, not in contention and envy:
texts.ESV: Let us walk properly as in the daytime, not in orgies and drunkenness, not in sexual immorality and sensuality, not in quarreling and jealousy.
texts.LEB: Let us live decently, as in the day, not in carousing and drunkenness, not in sexual immorality and licentiousness, not in strife and jealousy.
texts.AMP: Let us conduct ourselves properly and honorably as in the [light of] day, not in carousing and drunkenness, not in sexual promiscuity and irresponsibility, not in quarreling and jealousy.
texts.NLT: Because we belong to the day, we must live decent lives for all to see. Don’t participate in the darkness of wild parties and drunkenness, or in sexual promiscuity and immoral living, or in quarreling and jealousy.
texts.AKJV: Let us walk honestly, as in the day; not in rioting and drunkenness, not in chambering and wantonness, not in strife and envying.
texts.NRSV: let us walk decently as in the day, not in reveling and drunkenness, not in illicit sex and licentiousness, not in quarreling and jealousy.

texts.NIV84: Rather, clothe yourselves with the Lord Jesus Christ, and do not think about how to gratify the desires of the sinful nature.
texts.KJV: But put ye on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make not provision for the flesh, to fulfil the lusts thereof.
texts.NASB95: But put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the flesh in regard to its lusts.
texts.NKJV: But put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the flesh, to fulfill its lusts.
texts.DRB: But put ye on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make not provision for the flesh in its concupiscences.
texts.ESV: But put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the flesh, to gratify its desires.
texts.LEB: But put on the Lord Jesus Christ and do not make provision for the desires of the flesh.
texts.AMP: But clothe yourselves with the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for [nor even think about gratifying] the flesh in regard to its improper desires.
texts.NLT: Instead, clothe yourself with the presence of the Lord Jesus Christ. And don’t let yourself think about ways to indulge your evil desires.
texts.AKJV: But put ye on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make not provision for the flesh, to fulfil the lusts thereof.
texts.NRSV: Instead, put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the flesh, to gratify its desires.

texts.NIV84: They have become filled with every kind of wickedness, evil, greed and depravity. They are full of envy, murder, strife, deceit and malice. They are gossips,
texts.KJV: Being filled with all unrighteousness, fornication, wickedness, covetousness, maliciousness; full of envy, murder, debate, deceit, malignity; whisperers,
texts.NASB95: being filled with all unrighteousness, wickedness, greed, evil; full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, malice; they are gossips,
texts.NKJV: being filled with all unrighteousness, sexual immorality, wickedness, covetousness, maliciousness; full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, evil-mindedness; they are whisperers,
texts.DRB: Being filled with all iniquity, malice, fornication, avarice, wickedness, full of envy, murder, contention, deceit, malignity, whisperers,
texts.ESV: They were filled with all manner of unrighteousness, evil, covetousness, malice. They are full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, maliciousness. They are gossips,
texts.LEB: being filled with all unrighteousness, wickedness, greediness, malice, full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, malevolence. They are gossipers,
texts.AMP: until they were filled (permeated, saturated) with every kind of unrighteousness, wickedness, greed, evil; full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, malice and mean-spiritedness. They are gossips [spreading rumors],
texts.NLT: Their lives became full of every kind of wickedness, sin, greed, hate, envy, murder, quarreling, deception, malicious behavior, and gossip.
texts.AKJV: being filled with all unrighteousness, fornication, wickedness, covetousness, maliciousness; full of envy, murder, debate, deceit, malignity; whisperers,
texts.NRSV: They were filled with every kind of injustice, evil, covetousness, malice. Full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, craftiness, they are gossips,

texts.NIV84: slanderers, God-haters, insolent, arrogant and boastful; they invent ways of doing evil; they disobey their parents;
texts.KJV: Backbiters, haters of God, despiteful, proud, boasters, inventors of evil things, disobedient to parents,
texts.NASB95: slanderers, haters of God, insolent, arrogant, boastful, inventors of evil, disobedient to parents,
texts.NKJV: backbiters, haters of God, violent, proud, boasters, inventors of evil things, disobedient to parents,
texts.DRB: Detractors, hateful to God, contumelious, proud, haughty, inventors of evil things, disobedient to parents,
texts.ESV: slanderers, haters of God, insolent, haughty, boastful, inventors of evil, disobedient to parents,
texts.LEB: slanderers, haters of God, insolent, arrogant, boasters, contrivers of evil, disobedient to parents,
texts.AMP: slanderers, haters of God, insolent, arrogant, boastful, inventors [of new forms] of evil, disobedient and disrespectful to parents,
texts.NLT: They are backstabbers, haters of God, insolent, proud, and boastful. They invent new ways of sinning, and they disobey their parents.
texts.AKJV: backbiters, haters of God, despiteful, proud, boasters, inventors of evil things, disobedient to parents,
texts.NRSV: slanderers, God-haters, insolent, haughty, boastful, inventors of evil, rebellious toward parents,

texts.NIV84: they are senseless, faithless, heartless, ruthless.
texts.KJV: Without understanding, covenantbreakers, without natural affection, implacable, unmerciful:
texts.NASB95: without understanding, untrustworthy, unloving, unmerciful;
texts.NKJV: undiscerning, untrustworthy, unloving, unforgiving, unmerciful;
texts.DRB: Foolish, dissolute, without affection, without fidelity, without mercy.
texts.ESV: foolish, faithless, heartless, ruthless.
texts.LEB: senseless, faithless, unfeeling, unmerciful,
texts.AMP: without understanding, untrustworthy, unloving, unmerciful [without pity].
texts.NLT: They refuse to understand, break their promises, are heartless, and have no mercy.
texts.AKJV: without understanding, covenantbreakers, without natural affection, implacable, unmerciful:
texts.NRSV: foolish, faithless, heartless, ruthless.

texts.NIV84: Although they know God's righteous decree that those who do such things deserve death, they not only continue to do these very things but also approve of those who practice them.
texts.KJV: Who knowing the judgment of God, that they which commit such things are worthy of death, not only do the same, but have pleasure in them that do them.
texts.NASB95: and although they know the ordinance of God, that those who practice such things are worthy of death, they not only do the same, but also give hearty approval to those who practice them.
texts.NKJV: who, knowing the righteous judgment of God, that those who practice such things are deserving of death, not only do the same but also approve of those who practice them.
texts.DRB: Who, having known the justice of God, did not understand that they who do such things, are worthy of death; and not only they that do them, but they also that consent to them that do them.
texts.ESV: Though they know God's righteous decree that those who practice such things deserve to die, they not only do them but give approval to those who practice them.
texts.LEB: who, although they know the requirements of God, that those who do such things are worthy of death, not only do they do the same things, but also they approve of those who do them.
texts.AMP: Although they know God’s righteous decree and His judgment, that those who do such things deserve death, yet they not only do them, but they even [enthusiastically] approve and tolerate others who practice them.
texts.NLT: They know God’s justice requires that those who do these things deserve to die, yet they do them anyway. Worse yet, they encourage others to do them, too.
texts.AKJV: who knowing the judgment of God, that they which commit such things are worthy of death, not only do the same, but have pleasure in them that do them.
texts.NRSV: They know God’s decree, that those who practice such things deserve to die, yet they not only do them but even applaud others who practice them.

texts.NIV84: Who is wise and understanding among you? Let him show it by his good life, by deeds done in the humility that comes from wisdom.
texts.KJV: Who is a wise man and endued with knowledge among you? let him shew out of a good conversation his works with meekness of wisdom.
texts.NASB95: Who among you is wise and understanding? Let him show by his good behavior his deeds in the gentleness of wisdom.
texts.NKJV: Who is wise and understanding among you? Let him show by good conduct that his works are done in the meekness of wisdom.
texts.DRB: Who is a wise man, and endued with knowledge among you? Let him shew, by a good conversation, his work in the meekness of wisdom.
texts.ESV: Who is wise and understanding among you? By his good conduct let him show his works in the meekness of wisdom.
texts.LEB: Who is wise and understanding among you? Let him show by his good behavior his works, with the humility of wisdom.
texts.AMP: Who among you is wise and intelligent? Let him by his good conduct show his [good] deeds with the gentleness and humility of true wisdom.
texts.NLT: If you are wise and understand God’s ways, prove it by living an honorable life, doing good works with the humility that comes from wisdom.
texts.AKJV: Who is a wise man and endued with knowledge among you? let him shew out of a good conversation his works with meekness of wisdom.
texts.NRSV: Who is wise and knowledgeable among you? Show by your good life that your works are done with gentleness born of wisdom.

texts.NIV84: But if you harbor bitter envy and selfish ambition in your hearts, do not boast about it or deny the truth.
texts.KJV: But if ye have bitter envying and strife in your hearts, glory not, and lie not against the truth.
texts.NASB95: But if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your heart, do not be arrogant and so lie against the truth.
texts.NKJV: But if you have bitter envy and self-seeking in your hearts, do not boast and lie against the truth.
texts.DRB: But if you have bitter zeal, and there be contentions in your hearts; glory not, and be not liars against the truth.
texts.ESV: But if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your hearts, do not boast and be false to the truth.
texts.LEB: But if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your hearts, do not boast and tell lies against the truth.
texts.AMP: But if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your hearts, do not be arrogant, and [as a result] be in defiance of the truth.
texts.NLT: But if you are bitterly jealous and there is selfish ambition in your heart, don’t cover up the truth with boasting and lying.
texts.AKJV: But if ye have bitter envying and strife in your hearts, glory not, and lie not against the truth.
texts.NRSV: But if you have bitter envy and selfish ambition in your hearts, do not be arrogant and lie about the truth.

texts.NIV84: Such "wisdom" does not come down from heaven but is earthly, unspiritual, of the devil.
texts.KJV: This wisdom descendeth not from above, but is earthly, sensual, devilish.
texts.NASB95: This wisdom is not that which comes down from above, but is earthly, natural, demonic.
texts.NKJV: This wisdom does not descend from above, but is earthly, sensual, demonic.
texts.DRB: For this is not wisdom, descending from above: but earthly, sensual, devilish.
texts.ESV: This is not the wisdom that comes down from above, but is earthly, unspiritual, demonic.
texts.LEB: This is not the wisdom that comes down from above, but is earthly, unspiritual, demonic.
texts.AMP: This [superficial] wisdom is not that which comes down from above, but is earthly (secular), natural (unspiritual), even demonic.
texts.NLT: For jealousy and selfishness are not God’s kind of wisdom. Such things are earthly, unspiritual, and demonic.
texts.AKJV: This wisdom descendeth not from above, but is earthly, sensual, devilish.
texts.NRSV: This is not wisdom that comes down from above but is earthly, unspiritual, devilish.

texts.NIV84: For where you have envy and selfish ambition, there you find disorder and every evil practice.
texts.KJV: For where envying and strife is, there is confusion and every evil work.
texts.NASB95: For where jealousy and selfish ambition exist, there is disorder and every evil thing.
texts.NKJV: For where envy and self-seeking exist, confusion and every evil thing are there.
texts.DRB: For where envying and contention is, there is inconstancy, and every evil work.
texts.ESV: For where jealousy and selfish ambition exist, there will be disorder and every vile practice.
texts.LEB: For where there is jealousy and selfish ambition, there is disorder and every evil practice.
texts.AMP: For where jealousy and selfish ambition exist, there is disorder [unrest, rebellion] and every evil thing and morally degrading practice.
texts.NLT: For wherever there is jealousy and selfish ambition, there you will find disorder and evil of every kind.
texts.AKJV: For where envying and strife is, there is confusion and every evil work.
texts.NRSV: For where there is envy and selfish ambition, there will also be disorder and wickedness of every kind.

texts.NIV84: But the wisdom that comes from heaven is first of all pure; then peace-loving, considerate, submissive, full of mercy and good fruit, impartial and sincere.
texts.KJV: But the wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, and easy to be intreated, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality, and without hypocrisy.
texts.NASB95: But the wisdom from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, reasonable, full of mercy and good fruits, unwavering, without hypocrisy.
texts.NKJV: But the wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, willing to yield, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality and without hypocrisy.
texts.DRB: But the wisdom, that is from above, first indeed is chaste, then peaceable, modest, easy to be persuaded, consenting to the good, full of mercy and good fruits, without judging, without dissimulation.
texts.ESV: But the wisdom from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, open to reason, full of mercy and good fruits, impartial and sincere.
texts.LEB: But the wisdom from above is first pure, then peaceful, gentle, obedient, full of mercy and good fruits, nonjudgmental, without hypocrisy,
texts.AMP: But the wisdom from above is first pure [morally and spiritually undefiled], then peace-loving [courteous, considerate], gentle, reasonable [and willing to listen], full of compassion and good fruits. It is unwavering, without [self-righteous] hypocrisy [and self-serving guile].
texts.NLT: But the wisdom from above is first of all pure. It is also peace loving, gentle at all times, and willing to yield to others. It is full of mercy and the fruit of good deeds. It shows no favoritism and is always sincere.
texts.AKJV: But the wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, and easy to be intreated, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality, and without hypocrisy.
texts.NRSV: But the wisdom from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, willing to yield, full of mercy and good fruits, without a trace of partiality or hypocrisy.

texts.NIV84: Peacemakers who sow in peace raise a harvest of righteousness.
texts.KJV: And the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace of them that make peace.
texts.NASB95: And the seed whose fruit is righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.
texts.NKJV: Now the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.
texts.DRB: And the fruit of justice is sown in peace, to them that make peace.
texts.ESV: And a harvest of righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.
texts.LEB: And the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace among those who make peace.
texts.AMP: And the seed whose fruit is righteousness (spiritual maturity) is sown in peace by those who make peace [by actively encouraging goodwill between individuals].
texts.NLT: And those who are peacemakers will plant seeds of peace and reap a harvest of righteousness.
texts.AKJV: And the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace of them that make peace.
texts.NRSV: And the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.
